last_item: Band-1.5: wire the restore-test job into the runtime scheduler (background.go) so verify runs on a cadence, not just on a manual click
last_result: done — the verify-based Manager.RestoreTest (shipped iter 17) only ran on a manual 'Test a restore' click; the `restore-test` cron job was NEVER registered in startBackgroundJobs (only telemetry + full/incremental backups were), even though cfg.Schedules.RestoreTest (default `0 5 * * 0`, 05:00 Sun after the weekly full) already existed. Registered it via a NEW generic registerJob(name,spec,fn,emptyWarn) helper (refactored registerBackupJob to share it — DRY). scheduledRestoreTest calls s.backups.RestoreTest directly. KEY SAFETY DECISION (reviewer-caught, fixed pre-commit): it deliberately does NOT call ensureBackupConfigured like the backup jobs do — ensureBackupConfigured runs StanzaCreate (exclusive stanza lock) AND EnsureArchiving (may restart Postgres), either of which would COLLIDE with a 03:00 full backup still running (6h timeout) when verify fires at 05:00. Verify is a pure reader needing only the config the backup jobs already wrote. Reviewer also confirmed: verify needs no single-flight/CodeConflict guard (never writes repo), and returning a verify failure (vs swallowing) is correct for a scheduled durability check. Tests: restore-test registered alongside backup+telemetry jobs; empty schedule = operator opt-out (not registered). Full Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build); no web changes.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) NEARLY DONE. Done: (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced. (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP wired (background.go). (4) SCHEDULED BACKUPS RUN: full+incremental cron jobs; Manager.backupMu single-flight = CodeConflict skip on overlap (no fail row/false alert). (5) off-host S3 nudge test-locked. (6) restore-test SURFACING (restoreTestStatus + RestoreTestStatus banner). (7) ***restore-test EXECUTION DONE: Manager.RestoreTest runs `pgbackrest verify` (VerifyCmd, command.go verifyTimeout=2h); handler wired; recordRestoreTest helper mirrors recordBackup; verify is READ-ONLY so it does NOT take backupMu (correct — won't corrupt). verify ≠ full restore, so verified_rows stays 0 (honest).*** (8) ***restore-test JOB WIRED (iter 18): startBackgroundJobs now registers a `restore-test` cron job on cfg.Schedules.RestoreTest via a generic registerJob helper; scheduledRestoreTest calls Manager.RestoreTest directly and deliberately SKIPS ensureBackupConfigured (its stanza-create exclusive lock + possible PG restart would collide with a long full backup still running at 05:00) — verify is a pure reader.*** REMAINING 1.5: (a) the DEEP-proof follow-up (option c — non-destructive scratch restore + boot + REAL row count into verified_rows; catches recovery-time failures verify can't: WAL-replay gaps, corrupt pg_control). That one DOES need a disk-headroom precheck + guaranteed scratch cleanup + never-touch-live-dir + scratch-cluster-must-NOT-archive-into-the-live-repo + integration-tagged proof — its own backlog item, do NOT rush. (b) the `digest` job is also still unregistered, but NO digest builder exists yet — not load-bearing for 'never lose data', defer to band 2+. After that, band 1.5 is complete → move to band 2 (stability). Optionally wire OTLP exporter (nil in collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue. Backups: internal/backup/manager.go (Backup takes backupMu.TryLock→ownership→terminal fail/success; RestoreTest verifyForRead→VerifyCmd→pass/fail row); internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups/ListRestoreTests newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup, InsertRestoreTest). Frontend SUCCESS_RESULTS={success,ok,completed,pass}/FAILURE_RESULTS={fail,failed,error} in Backups.tsx — Manager writes 'success'/'fail'. Scheduler: internal/scheduler — empty spec disables. go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
